message.content: @client\src\components\Universe\Universe.jsx during the mobile version the ._linkModeButton_126bf_845 is covered by ._distanceTracker_126bf_99. they stuck together, they should be not together stucking to each other can you fix that lay out? /frontend-design

content.thinking: The user wants me to fix a layout issue on mobile where the link mode button and distance tracker are overlapping/sticking together. Let me look at the CSS module to understand the current styles.

content.text: Let me look at the CSS module to understand the current layout.

content.thinking:
Now I can see the issue. On mobile (max-width: 600px), both `.linkModeButton` and `.distanceTracker` are positioned at:
- `.linkModeButton`: `top: 12px; right: 12px;`
- `.distanceTracker`: `top: 12px; right: 12px;`

They're both in the top-right corner, overlapping each other. I need to separate them.

The back button is at `top: 12px; left: 12px;`. The link mode button is moved to `top: 12px; right: 12px;`. And the distance tracker is also moved to `top: 12px; right: 12px;`.


Since they're stacking on top of each other, I should move the distance tracker down below the link mode button instead, keeping it on the right side but giving it vertical space so both elements are visible.

content.text:
I can see the problem. On mobile, both `.linkModeButton` and `.distanceTracker` are positioned at `top: 12px; right: 12px;` — they land right on top of each other.

I'll move the distance tracker down so it sits below the link mode button.

content.content: The file C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\Universe\Universe.module.css has been updated successfully.

content.text: The distance tracker is now at `top: 52px` on mobile, which places it below the back button and link mode button row (both at `top: 12px`). They'll no longer overlap.
